title: epipactis parviflora
intro: Epipactis parviflora, also known as the lesser helleborine, is a terrestrial orchid native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including the ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. Epipactis parviflora can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
